assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response clearly identifies the issue as cert-manager webhook being incompatible with K8s 1.32 and explains this causes admission webhook errors preventing pod creation

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Provides commands 'kubectl get validatingadmissionwebhooks | grep cert-manager' and 'kubectl get mutatingadmissionwebhooks | grep cert-manager' to identify the problematic webhooks

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Explicitly offers temporary disabling of the webhook as 'Option 1: Temporarily disable the webhook' in the Immediate Fix section

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Provides specific kubectl delete commands: 'kubectl delete validatingadmissionwebhook cert-manager-webhook' and 'kubectl delete mutatingadmissionwebhook cert-manager-webhook'

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: While the response mentions this is temporary and provides a 'Proper Fix' section, it doesn't explicitly warn about the security implications of removing validation or that this removes cert-manager's validation capabilities

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Step 1 of the Proper Fix section explicitly states 'Check cert-manager version compatibility' and provides commands to check the current version

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Step 2 provides commands to upgrade cert-manager and mentions checking compatibility with 1.32 (though the expectation says 1.30, the response correctly addresses 1.32)

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: The response does not mention modifying failurePolicy to Ignore as an alternative to deletion - it only provides deletion as the immediate fix option

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Step 5 includes verification commands: 'kubectl run test-pod --image=nginx --rm -it -- echo "success"' to test pod creation after the fix
